content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 25, 1989

Lance Olson
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-471

Dear Mr. Olson:

You have asked for confirmation of our telephone conversation
concerning the date that the opinion of the Court of Appeal in
Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. Fair Political Practices
Commission (2d Civ. No. B039177) becomes "final." After I
received your letter, I informed you that we are unable to provide
written confirmation of that conversation at this time, pending
resolution of the question in the form of a Commission opinion.

Attached is a copy of the opinion request and the draft
opinion, which will be considered at the Commission meeting to be
held September 6, 1989.

If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED/aa

Attachments

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

August 11, 1989

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR FORMAL WRITTEN ADVICE

Dear Ms. Donovan:

On behalf of our numerous clients involved in
legislative campaigns, this letter is to confirm our
recent telephone conversation in which you stated that
the Commission believes that the court of Appeal
decision in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v.
Fair Political Practices Commission would become
operative no earlier than October 1, 1989.

In our telephone conversation, you stated that you
had several conversations with the Court of Appeal and
that they had advised you that October 1, 1989, would
be the earliest date. Our office then had two
conversations with the clerk of the Division which
rendered the decision. He also advised us of that
date; however, when we pointed out that the first of
October falls on a Sunday, he concurred that the
earliest date on which the remittitur could issue would
be Monday, October 2, 1989.

Because the ban on non-election year fundraising
will take effect once the decision becomes operative,
it is imperative that our clients know what time
period is in which they may safely plan on raising or
contributing funds. Each of the clients on whose
behalf this advice is requested plans on receiving
(either as candidate or treasurer) or contributing to
legislative candidates between now and January 1, 1990.
For that reason, we would greatly appreciate a prompt
response to this request for formal written advice.

A list of the clients on whose behalf this advice
is sought is attached hereto as Exhibit "A."

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

LANCE H. OLSON

CC: All Clients Listed in Exhibit "A"

EXHIBIT "A"

California Democratic Party
Rusty Areias
Bruce Bronzan
Steve Clute
Lloyd Connelly
Delaine Eastin
Dick Floyd
John Garamendi
Leroy Greene
Tom Hannigan
Phil Isenberg
Jack O'Connell
Lon Hatamiya
Association of California State Attorneys and
Administrative Law Judges PAC
Cal-Hy-PAC (California Dental Hygienists PAC)
California School Employees PACE
United Food and Commercial Workers PAC
Communications Workers of America PAC
Sacramento Womens Campaign Fund
California Water Association PAC
PAWPAC
Harry S. Truman Club
P.O.R.A.C.
CACJ PAC (California Attorneys for Criminal Justice)
OAAC PAC (Outdoor Advertising Council)
Laborers Local 185 PAC
Better Health Care of Memorial Services PAC
Lance H. Olson, Treasurer
Suzette Skill Olson, Treasurer

sections.question: On behalf of our numerous clients involved in legislative campaigns, this letter is to confirm our recent telephone conversation in which you stated that the Commission believes that the Court of Appeal decision in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign spending v. Fair Political Practices Commission would become operative no earlier than October 1, 1989. Because the ban on non-election year fundraising will take effect once the decision becomes operative, it is imperative that our clients know what the time period is in which they may safely plan on raising or contributing funds.
sections.question_synthetic: What is the effective date when the Court of Appeal decision in Taxpayers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC becomes operative, so that clients can determine the timeframe for non-election year fundraising?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The FPPC response letter is also included in the document, dated August 25, 1989, which declines to provide written confirmation pending a Commission opinion. The incoming request is the primary document being analyzed per the document structure.

embedding.summary: Lance Olson requests formal written advice from FPPC General Counsel Kathryn Donovan regarding the operative date of a Court of Appeal decision on campaign spending, which will trigger a ban on non-election year fundraising. The request seeks clarity on whether October 1 or October 2, 1989 is the effective date, on behalf of numerous legislative candidates and PACs.
